title: CypherX Safety Classify API (Contract 2.x)
description: Request/response contract for the safety/moderation classifier exposed by the Guardrails service. Classifies a single text payload, on either the inbound (input) or outbound (output) direction, into a moderation verdict plus per-category scores. Forward-compatible: every object sets additionalProperties:true and requires only validation-mandatory fields, so later phases may add optional fields without a breaking v2. The CLASSIFIER_MODE flag defaults to 'stub'; the stub classifier returns verdict=allow with empty/low category scores, reproducing today's permissive behaviour when no real model is configured.

input.description: The text payload to classify for safety/moderation.

direction.description: Whether the payload is the request `input` to the model or the `output` returned from it (mirrors Contract 5 guardrails.violation.detected.direction).

context.description: Optional caller-supplied context for the classification (e.g. tenant policy id, conversation excerpt, locale). Open-ended by design.

verdict.description: The moderation decision. `allow` passes unchanged; `warn` passes but flags; `redact` requires masking before passing; `block` denies (maps to a GUARDRAIL_VIOLATION on enforcement).

categories.description: Per-category safety scores that informed the verdict. May be empty when nothing of concern was detected (e.g. the stub classifier).

score.description: Confidence in [0,1] that the payload matches this category.

scores.description: Optional flat map of category name -> score, as a convenience alongside the structured `categories` array. Open-ended by design.

model.description: The classifier model that produced this verdict (e.g. `omni-moderation`, or `stub` in CLASSIFIER_MODE=stub / keyless local dev).
